data.title: The Mosaic Company is the most oversold materials stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most oversold stocks in the materials sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 materials stocks ranked based on their lowest Relative Strength Index.

The list is topped by The Mosaic Company (MOS), with an RSI of 41. LyondellBasell Industries (LYB) and CF Industries Holdings (CF) are next, with Corteva (CTVA) and Dow Inc. (DOW) rounding out the rest of the top five.

Large-cap names like Corteva (CTVA) at $53.90B and Dow Inc. (DOW) at $26.46B feature prominently on the list. Other industries represented include forest products with Louisiana-Pacific Corporation (LPX) and silver with Hecla Mining Company (HL).

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

RSI readings of 70 or above are generally considered a signal that a stock may be overbought and potentially poised for a pullback, while readings below 30 typically signal oversold conditions.

Here is the list:

  1. The Mosaic Company (MOS), Market Cap: $7.66B, RSI: 41

  2. LyondellBasell Industries N.V. (LYB), Market Cap: $22.14B, RSI: 44

  3. CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (CF), Market Cap: $17.81B, RSI: 45

  4. Corteva, Inc. (CTVA), Market Cap: $53.90B, RSI: 45

  5. Dow Inc. (DOW), Market Cap: $26.46B, RSI: 46

  6. Louisiana-Pacific Corporation (LPX), Market Cap: $5.15B, RSI: 47

  7. Olin Corporation (OLN), Market Cap: $3.06B, RSI: 48

  8. Warrior Met Coal, Inc. (HCC), Market Cap: $4.62B, RSI: 48

  9. Crown Holdings, Inc. (CCK), Market Cap: $11.57B, RSI: 48

  10. Hecla Mining Company (HL), Market Cap: $12.96B, RSI: 49

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), and (RSPM)

data.title: AI, chip stocks mixed as ceasefire deadline looms amid uncertain peace talks
data.description:
Chip and AI-related stocks were mixed on Tuesday as the truce in the U.S.-Israel-Iran war remains uncertain with the ceasefire deadline looming.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell around 0.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) also declined about 0.3%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) also dipped nearly 0.2%.

U.S. President Donald Trump said today that he does not want to extend the new Iran ceasefire deadline of Wednesday evening and noted that he expects "to be bombing" if a deal isn’t reached, CNN reported.

Vice President JD Vance, who was to travel to Pakistan for peace talks, delayed his departure, the New York Times reported, citing a U.S. official. Iran has not publicly confirmed that its negotiators will attend.

Brent Futures (CO1:COM) and Crude Oil Futures (CL1:COM) each climbed about 3.9% on Tuesday.

Meanwhile, Kevin Warsh, the former Fed governor whom President Donald Trump nominated as Federal Reserve chair, faced questions on Tuesday during his confirmation hearings in the Senate. The confirmation hearing started at 10 a.m. ET.

Shares of AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) fell about 1%. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) jumped nearly 3%, while Broadcom (AVGO) was also in the green. Qualcomm (QCOM) dipped about 1%.

Several other AI and networking-related stocks were mixed. Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) and Lumentum (LITE) each tumbled about 5%, while Coherent (COHR) was largely flat but in the red. Arista Networks (ANET) climbed around 2%, while Cisco (CSCO) and Ciena (CIEN) each rose about 1%. Corning (GLW) and Celestica (CLS) were also in the green.

Meanwhile, Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) fell about 2%, and Arm (ARM), GlobalFoundries (GFS), Analog Devices (ADI), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), and Texas Instruments (TXN) each declined nearly 1%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) rose about 2%, while Intel (INTC) and Micron Technology (MU) were largely flat but in the green.

Chip equipment makers: ASML (ASML), Lam Research (LRCX), and KLA (KLAC) each fell nearly 1%. However, Applied Materials (AMAT) seemed to buck the trend in this space and rose about 1%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Kforce gains after William Blair upgrade on staffing demand outlook
data.description:
Shares of Kforce (KFRC) rose 5.17% to $32.52 after William Blair upgraded the stock to “Outperform,” citing signs of improvement in the U.S. professional staffing market.

The brokerage said recent data points suggest demand may be stabilizing after a prolonged downturn, noting that “overall staffing demand has improved or at least stabilized in recent months” and that the sector could offer upside tied to a cyclical recovery.

Blair pointed to multiple indicators, including improving job postings and stronger trends in temporary staffing, adding that “we now have a confluence of indicators suggesting that U.S. temporary staffing has bottomed.”

The firm also highlighted earnings leverage potential as demand recovers, stating that “earnings power at these companies is spring-loaded to grow as revenue rebounds.”

On valuation, the brokerage said sentiment remains weak despite improving fundamentals, noting that “risk/reward [is] too compelling to ignore, with sentiment too negative,” and sees potential upside for Kforce over the next year.

data.title: UPS expands its Happy Returns network to over 10K locations
data.description:
United Parcel Service (UPS) and Happy Returns expanded their box-free, label-free Return Bar network to 10K U.S. locations. The expansion reinforces the partnership's standing as the country’s largest consolidated return network. 

The milestone came after more than 1,700 new sites were added, primarily through partnerships with Annex Brands and PackageHub Business Centers, building on existing relationships with The UPS Store, Staples, and Ulta Beauty (ULTA) to give online shoppers more convenient return options and immediate refunds.

The expanded network means 79% of the U.S. population now lives within five miles of a Return Bar, and more than a quarter lives within one mile, which UPS (UPS) highlights as making returns easier to integrate into routine errands. As part of the returns program, shoppers can bring unpackaged items without labels to participating locations, where associates scan a barcode to verify the return and trigger an instant refund, eliminating the need for boxes, printers, or lengthy in-store processes.

"Our Return Bar network is now more than three times the size of the next closest option, significantly expanding access to box-free, label-free returns with immediate refunds. This growth allows us to deliver unmatched convenience to online shoppers across the country," updated Happy Returns CEO David Sobie.

Happy Returns is a reverse logistics and technology company that provides end-to-end returns solutions for online retailers and their customers, focused on box-free, label-free in-person drop-offs and streamlined processing of e-commerce returns. Founded in 2015 and headquartered in Santa Monica, California, it operates as a UPS company and works with brands such as Everlane, SHEIN, and REVOLVE to make returns faster, less costly, and more sustainable. UPS (UPS) acquired Happy Returns from PayPal in late 2023.

data.title: Spirit Airlines soars as Trump pushes for buyer for distressed airline
data.description:
Spirit Airlines (FLLYQ) jumped 156% as President Donald Trump said he would like to see a buyer for the distressed airline and said he may be open to the government helping out the discount carrier. 

'I’d love somebody to buy Spirit,” Trump said Tuesday in an interview with CNBC. "Spirit's in trouble. Maybe the federal government should help that one out."

Trump's comments come after a Bloomberg report on Monday that Spirit Aviation Holdings (FLYYQ) was looking for an infusion of cash from the U.S. government in a late bid to prevent a full liquidation of the airline. Sources told Bloomberg that an offer has been made to government officials.

Spirit Airlines (FLYYQ) is currently moving through a second Chapter 11 bankruptcy. The airline company had previously signaled that it expected to emerge from bankruptcy by early summer, and it had reached agreements aimed at keeping the process on track. However, if the airline cannot secure a workable deal with lenders and other stakeholders, liquidation could become a last-resort option to wind down the business and sell off assets.

Trump also said he would be against a combination of United Airlines (UAL) and American Airlines (AAL) after reports last week about a possible combination.

"I don't mind mergers," Trump said in the interview. "But with American, it's doing fine and United is doing very well. I know the United people; they are doing very well. I don't like having them merge."

American Airlines (AAL) announced on Friday that it had not engaged in discussions with United Airlines (UAL) regarding a merger and that it was not interested in such a deal.

data.title: Harmony Bio spikes after new Wakix patent litigation
data.description:
• Shares of Harmony Biosciences (HRMY) climbed on Tuesday after Mizuho issued bullish remarks on the stock, citing a new patent litigation filed by the company to fight generics targeting its sleep disorder therapy, Wakix.
  • Analyst Graig Suvannavejh noted that the lawsuit filed on April 20 against privately held AET Pharma and its commercial partner Sandoz (SDZNY) (SDZXF) alleges infringement of a '920 patent covering Wakix, Harmony’s (HRMY) only approved product.
  • The development is a “significant positive” for the company, Suvannavejh argued, noting that it will help Harmony (HRMY) remove a major overhang that implied a potential market entry of Wakix generics well before 2030.
  • “Given this most recent legal salvo from HRMY, we believe HRMY's Wakix franchise should now be viewed as being protected and fully intact until 2030,” the analyst wrote, reiterating his Outperform rating and $50 target on the stock.

data.title: Vance’s Islamabad trip on hold as Iran fails to respond to U.S. terms
data.description:
Vice President JD Vance’s diplomatic trip to Islamabad, Pakistan, has been put on hold after Tehran failed to respond to American negotiating positions, according to the New York Times, citing a U.S. official with direct knowledge of the situation.

The official said, “The diplomatic process is in effect paused, though the trip has not been canceled.”

Vance had been scheduled to depart Tuesday morning for the Pakistani capital, where talks were set to resume Wednesday—the same day the fragile cease-fire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire.

The trip could be back on at a moment’s notice if Iran’s negotiators respond in a way that President Trump deems acceptable.

U.S. officials are also looking for a “clear signal” that Iran’s negotiators have been fully empowered to reach an agreement, while the Pentagon has been reviewing military options should Trump conclude that Tehran is not negotiating in good faith.

The U.S. recently transmitted a written proposal to the Iranians intended to establish baseline points of agreement that could frame more detailed negotiations.

The core sticking points remain the same issues that have challenged Western negotiators for more than a decade: the scope of Iran’s uranium enrichment program and the fate of its stockpile of enriched uranium.

Earlier, U.S. President Donald Trump told CNBC that he expects the U.S. to “end up with a great deal” with Iran to end the war. He also said he does not expect to extend the ongoing ceasefire, which will expire on Wednesday.

More on the U.S.-Iran war

  • JD Vance, U.S. delegation to land in Pakistan on Wednesday for talks with Iran- reports
  • Trump signals no extension of truce ahead of scheduled peace talks; oil down
  • Trump threatens again to blow up all Iran's bridges and power plants
  • When will Hormuz shipping normalize? See what traders are predicting

data.title: Texas Instruments Q1 Preview: AI demand, industrial rebound in focus
data.description:
When Texas Instruments (TXN) reports its first-quarter earnings on Wednesday, Wall Street expects the chip maker to post an EPS of $1.37, while revenue is expected to rise 11.3% to $4.53B for the quarter.

The chip maker, in the previous quarter, missed both revenue and EPS estimates but guided Q1 estimates above market consensus on the back of improving orders throughout the quarter and stronger bookings.

The company expects Q1 revenue in the range of $4.32 billion to $4.68 billion and earnings per share in the range of $1.22 to $1.48.

Mizuho upgraded Texas Instruments to Neutral from Underperform and increased its price target to $215 from $160.

The brokerage firm noted that NVDA GPU and custom ASIC (application-specific integrated circuit) push to 800V/48V/12V DC AI server architectures is driving upside to power analog demand for TXN.

Analysts led by Vijay Rakesh believe strong AI server and industrial demand are driving TXN and other analog suppliers to raise prices in China over 10% in April (a big positive driving JunQ upside).

In addition, the analysts believe that Texas Instruments is a beneficiary of an industrial recovery, and recent segment-specific price hikes of over 10% for stock-keeping units, or SKUs, in power semis for AI servers are driving further upside.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating are bullish, rating TXN a Buy and above. On the other hand,  Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious, rating it a Hold.

Seeking Alpha analyst Grassroots Trading noted that TXN's near-term growth is driven by data center and automotive segments, while industrial demand remains ~25% below prior peak and is due for a rebound.

“However, China is becoming both a major growth opportunity and an increasingly competitive market, with local manufacturers offering considerably lower prices than their competitors,” the analyst added.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen eight upward revisions and no downward moves, while revenue estimates have seen 18 upward revisions versus four downward revisions.

Texas Instruments’ shares have gained nearly 35% year-to-date, outperforming the benchmark S&P 500, which has risen nearly 4% in the same period.

data.title: Southern First Bancshares GAAP EPS of $1.19, revenue of $33.8M
data.description:
• Southern First Bancshares press release (SFST): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.19.
  • Revenue of $33.8M.
  • Net interest income improved by $6.9 million, or 29% year-over-year, driven primarily by new loan volume
  • Net interest margin was 2.88%, a 16 basis point increase from 2.72% for the fourth quarter of 2025, and included a $543 thousand repayment of interest on one large nonaccrual loan
  • Noninterest income was $3.5 million compared to $3.1 million for the first quarter last year; the increase was impacted by a one-time $515 thousand loss on the sale of securities in the fourth quarter of 2025
  • Noninterest expense to average assets was 1.84%, compared to 1.87% for first quarter of 2025
  • Return on average equity was 10.67%, compared to 6.38% for the first quarter of 2025
  • Return on average assets was 0.91%, compared to 0.52% for the first quarter of 2025

data.title: Apple praised by Needham analyst over CEO switch; firm pounds the table on Disney deal
data.description:
Following the surprising timing of Apple (AAPL) naming John Ternus as its next CEO, succeeding Tim Cook, a number of Wall Street analysts and investors have praised the tech giant for the move.

Count Needham analyst Laura Martin among those championing the decision, even as she repeatedly pushed Apple to aggressively move away from its core ethos and delve deeper into advertising and M&A.

“We are supportive of the succession plan announced yesterday by AAPL, whereby Tim Cook will leave as CEO on Sept. 1 (to become Executive Chairman of the Board), and John Ternus, currently SVP of Hardware Engineering, will become the CEO of AAPL,” Martin wrote in a note to clients. “We are optimistic about value creation from this transition because we believe Ternus will bring a sense of urgency (i.e., speed) and risk-taking (i.e., innovation) to AAPL's product line.”

Martin, who has a rare Hold rating on Apple, continued to pound the table that Apple move further into advertising. She went so far as to say that she believes the company “has been destroying shareholder value by underexploiting high-margin advertising [revenues].”

In her estimate, Apple generated roughly $10B in ad revenue in 2025, less than 10% of revenue from its Services segment and less than 3% of total revenue. “We believe this revenue stream should be closer to ~50% of Services revs and, at ~80% margins, a key upside profit driver for AAPL,” Martin added.

Last month, Apple opened up its near-ubiquitous Maps app to advertising and recently expanded advertising inside the App Store.

Martin also said that Apple needs to buy (or at least partner with) media giant Walt Disney (DIS), a deal she has repeatedly championed to no avail.

“We believe AAPL should partner with, or buy, Disney, in order to drive longer engagement lengths and give it differentiated assets (i.e., films and TV series) that have pricing power and powerful moats,” Martin explained. “We believe AAPL should also be using M&A, partnerships, and industry leadership to accelerate value creation.”

The two companies have a long-standing relationship. Apple co-founder Steve Jobs became the largest Disney shareholder after he sold Pixar to Disney in 2006.

Apple shares fell 2.5% in midday trading on Tuesday.
